decision: Sign-off requested 23 May on Option C: notify warranty claims into W&I, ringfence fraud as personal DLA claim, injunct non-compete urgently, reconcile FY2026 earn-out pre-issue. Quantum £3.4m–£3.85m inside £4m cap; long-stop 14 August.
rendered:
Decision: Option C sign-off by 23 May. Six authorisations at paragraph 70 of the underlying memo. Long-stop 14 August on warranty notification.

Top risks
1. Notification deadline 14 August 2026. Hard. RWE Nukem and Laminates Acquisition: non-compliant notification is fatal irrespective of merits. Three notification letters (Belmont, DLA, RouteCore) on a tight critical path. Action: drafts 6 June, service 18 July, four weeks of slack only.
2. W&I cover vs fraud pleading interaction. Markel policy excludes pre-completion seller fraud. The DLA reclassification is the fraud-shaped piece. If we plead fraud on the warranty claim, the £4m W&I primary on Belmont, HMRC and RouteCore is at risk. Strategic answer: ringfence fraud as a personal claim against Lockwood limited to the £180k DLA loss (uncapped by clause 9.10 carve-out, but uninsured). Keep W&I cover clean on the other three issues.
3. Lockwood earn-out counterclaim has independent substance. Schedule 7 paragraph 4 excludes one-off restructuring costs from earn-out accounts. Northshore's loading of integration costs into FY2025 is arguably mis-attributed and properly belongs in FY2026 — which would put FY2026 EBITDA below the £2m trigger. Worth up to £2m to Lockwood. Reconcile with Atkins Bramwell before any SPA claim is filed.

Escalate
- IC briefing only at this stage. LP-level disclosure deferred pending quantum clarity.
- Markel claims handling — they will direct strategy on insured claims; expect Peters Bramwell QC if it proceeds. Linklaters Disputes monitoring.
- Counsel: Joshua Park KC (Fountain Court) SPA/fraud; Sarah Mitchell (11 Stone Buildings) injunction.
- Marsden & Bell separate professional negligence claim — hold in reserve.

Settled
- All four warranty issues clearly breached on documentary record.
- Aggregate quantum £2.94m–£3.21m on warranties; £460k–£640k on tax indemnity; £3.4m–£3.85m total. Inside the £4m warranty cap.
- W&I cover £4m primary, £42.5k retention, IP/licensing sub-limit £2m, tax sub-limit £2m. Belmont and HMRC notifications already with Markel; RouteCore due 28 May.
- Tax indemnity 7-year notification window to 14 Feb 2032 — no time pressure on the HMRC point.
- No conflict. Eversfield Quinn conflicted out (written confirmation 28 April 2026).

Partner judgment required on
- Whether the Marsden & Bell professional negligence claim is genuinely held in reserve or signalled to them now (signalling now may produce earlier disclosure but tips defensive posture; recommendation: hold).
- Posture on Lockwood's consultancy summary termination claim. Risk three months' fees in lieu (£37,500) plus statutory if held wrongful. Small money but a clean loss is bad optics.
- Whether to commission the FY2026 earn-out reconciliation through Atkins Bramwell (Northshore's choice) or jointly with Marsden & Bell (more credible to Lockwood, slower). Recommendation: Atkins Bramwell, share findings only when notification served.
